name: Morton Gould
description: American composer, conductor, arranger, and pianist (1913–1996)
abstract: Morton Gould (December 10, 1913 – February 21, 1996) was an American composer, conductor, arranger, and pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: “The Latin American Symphonette” by Morton Gould. And it was a duet between alto sax and oboe, my oboe teacher. That’s 1951. And I never forgot it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: Morton Gould was the one that asked me to come up as his guest one night up at the Top of the Theatre and when I went I took the band and played.

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Morton Gould used to call me and say, “I’m recording next week I need Doc, I need Bobby — fix Tuesday and Wednesday so I can have them.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: We were playing the Morton Gould Pavanne... Mr. Brown said, 'Boy, that’s not the way the bass line goes.'
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Morton Gould wrote me a nice little piece called called Guajiro . . . Guajira, in 7/4.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
